title: What to know about Euphrasia officinalis before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Euphrasia officinalis are for the treatment of eye conditions, particularly dry eye, due to its ability to increase tear production and reduce inflammation. Euphrasia officinalis contains flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in alleviating pink eye and conjunctivitis. Red eyes are also relieved by Euphrasia officinalis, thanks to its ability to reduce vascular permeability and alleviate symptoms of allergic conjunctivitis, such as itching and swelling. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also make it useful for treating eye inflammation, which can lead to secondary conditions like glaucoma, for which Euphrasia officinalis may help lower intraocular pressure. Additionally, Euphrasia officinalis can help alleviate eye strain and infections, and its antiseptic properties can soothe itchy eyes.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Euphrasia officinalis are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which prevents the growth of microorganisms that cause infections. The antioxidant properties of this plant play a crucial role in scavenging free radicals and protecting cells from oxidative damage, thereby contributing to its anti-inflammatory effects. Euphrasia officinalis also exhibits a vasoconstrictor effect, which is likely due to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. Additionally, the antihistamine properties of this plant help alleviate allergic reactions, and its analgesic properties make it a potential natural pain reliever. The pupillary dilator effect of Euphrasia officinalis is primarily attributed to the presence of volatile oils, particularly nepetalactone, which is responsible for its medicinal properties.
identification: Euphrasia officinalis is a small, delicate, perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, typically growing to 5-20 cm in height, with slender stems and ovate to lanceolate leaves that are usually 1-6 cm long and 0.5-1.5 cm wide, often forming rosettes. This plant is native to Europe, but has been introduced to and naturalized in other parts of the world, including North America, and is commonly found in moist, grassy areas, particularly in wet meadows and along streams. The plant's flowers are usually pink or purple, with a tube-shaped calyx and a trumpet-shaped corolla that is often 6-10 mm long and 2-4 mm wide, with four stamens and two styles. Euphrasia officinalis is typically found in areas with high humidity and moderate temperatures, often between 10-25°C, and can thrive in a variety of soils, from acidic to slightly alkaline. Its geographical distribution ranges from Arctic tundra to subalpine zones, and it is commonly found in mountainous regions, where it can grow in crevices and rocky outcrops.
cultivation: Euphrasia officinalis is a temperate perennial plant that thrives in acidic soils with a pH between 4.5 and 6.5, typically found in wet meadows, grasslands, and along streams. It grows to a height of 5-20 cm, forming clumps through stolons and rhizomes, and exhibits a growth habit of semi-erect to prostrate stems with a high degree of branching. Optimal growth is achieved in partial shade to full sun, with temperatures between 5-25°C and annual precipitation ranging from 600-1200 mm. To promote healthy growth, it is recommended to maintain moist but not waterlogged soils, avoid over-fertilization, and provide regular pruning to control stolons and encourage new growth. Cultivation in well-drained acidic soils and with a moderate level of moisture can lead to a successful and sustainable harvest of Euphrasia officinalis.
history: Euphrasia officinalis is a plant traditionally used in herbal medicine, primarily for its eye remedies, dating back to the 16th century. The plant's leaves and flowers contain iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its pharmacological effects. It has been employed to treat conditions such as conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and uveitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional European folk medicine, Euphrasia officinalis was used to treat various eye problems, and it was also used as a treatment for colds and fever. In the 19th century, it was officially listed in the British Pharmacopoeia and was used in pharmaceutical preparations.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are phenolic compounds with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids found in Euphrasia officinalis are flavonoids, cinnamic acid derivatives and hydroxybenzoic acid derivatives, which are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are triterpene glycosides found in Euphrasia officinalis with surfactant, foaming, and hemolytic properties.

constituents.name: Iridoids
constituents.description: Iridoids are a group of compounds found in Euphrasia officinalis, characterized by their iridane skeleton, with reported properties including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Alkaloids in Euphrasia officinalis include euphrasin, which exhibits spasmolytic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, and euphorin, which exhibits spasmolytic and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Quercetin
constituents.description: Quercetin is a flavonoid constituent of Euphrasia officinalis, characterized by antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Glycosides are a type of compound found in Euphrasia officinalis, characterized by their glycosidic bonds and the presence of a sugar moiety.

constituents.name: Kaempferol
constituents.description: Kaempferol is a flavonoid glycoside found in the plant Euphrasia officinalis, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties.

constituents.name: Tannins
constituents.description: Tannins in Euphrasia officinalis are astringent, antiseptic and antimicrobial constituents.

constituents.name: Triterpenoids
constituents.description: A group of compounds found in Euphrasia officinalis, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are fragrant, phenolic compounds that have antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Rutin
constituents.description: Rutin is a bioflavonoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and it has been found to have vasoprotective, hemostatic, and antihistaminic effects.

constituents.name: Iridoid glycosides
constituents.description: Iridoid glycosides are bitter-tasting, iridoid compounds with hepatoprotective and spasmolytic properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic Acids
constituents.description: Phenolic Acids are found in Euphrasia officinalis and have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties.

constituents.name: Luteolin
constituents.description: A flavonoid constituent of Euphrasia officinalis, characterized by its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenes
constituents.description: Euphrasia officinalis triterpenes are compounds that exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Rosmarinic acid
constituents.description: A polyphenolic compound that exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Hyperoside
constituents.description: Hyperoside is a flavonoid glycoside with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Terpenoids are present in Euphrasia officinalis, possessing anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Phenylethanoids
constituents.description: Phenylethanoids are a type of bioactive compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Consuming large amounts of Euphrasia officinalis causes severe gastrointestinal symptoms, including vomiting, diarrhea and abdominal pain.
Consuming Euphrasia officinalis may cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in some individuals.
Euphrasia officinalis tincture causes headache due to the presence of pseudoalkaloids, primarily euphrasine and anglesitine.
Euphrasia officinalis contains euphrasine, a stimulant that affects blood pressure, causing a sudden drop resulting in dizziness.
Euphrasia officinalis causes nausea due to its iridoid glycosides.
Euphrasia officinalis ingestion causes gastric irritation leading to vomiting
Euphrasia officinalis may cause gastrointestinal irritation leading to abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting.
Euphrasia officinalis has been associated with fatigue in some individuals who ingest it.
Euphrasia officinalis ingestion causes an increase in heart rate.
Euphrasia officinalis ingestion causes a decrease in blood pressure.

intro_description: Euphrasia officinalis, commonly known as eyebright, is a herb used to treat various health conditions including conjunctivitis, sinusitis, and respiratory infections. Its main health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and improving vision. The herb's therapeutic properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antihistamine effects. Euphrasia officinalis contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. People often use the herb to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat eye and respiratory problems.

uses_list.description: This herb is used to treat dry eye due to its ability to increase tear production and reduce inflammation in the eye, thus providing relief from dryness and irritation.

It is used to treat conjunctivitis, an inflammation or infection of the conjunctiva, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with the condition.

This herb is used to treat pink eye, a common name for conjunctivitis, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and microbial infections of the eye, thus providing relief from redness, itching, and discharge.
There is no significant evidence to support the use of this herb in the treatment of glaucoma, a group of eye conditions that damage the optic nerve, often due to abnormally high pressure in the eye.

It is used to treat uveitis, an inflammation of the uvea, the middle layer of the eye, due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness, pain, and sensitivity associated with the condition.

This herb is used to treat sinusitis, an inflammation or infection of the sinus cavities, due to its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion, sinus pressure, and pain associated with the condition.

It is used to treat iritis, an inflammation of the iris, the colored part of the eye, due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness, pain, and sensitivity associated with the condition.

This herb is used to treat keratitis, an inflammation of the cornea, the transparent outer layer of the eye, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce pain, redness, and discharge associated with the condition.
It is used to treat inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with the condition.
There is no significant evidence to support the use of this herb in the treatment of eczema, a group of skin conditions characterized by dry, itchy, and inflamed skin.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Euphrasia officinalis is used to treat dry eye, a condition where the eyes do not produce enough tears to keep them lubricated and comfortable. It has also been used to alleviate uveitis, an inflammation of the uvea, the middle layer of the eye, which can cause pain and vision problems. Additionally, it is applied to conjunctivitis, a red and itchy eye condition caused by an infection or allergy, as well as glaucoma, a condition where the pressure in the eye increases, potentially leading to vision loss. Furthermore, Euphrasia officinalis is used to treat pink eye, sinusitis, eczema, keratitis, inflammation, meibomian gland dysfunction, allergic conjunctivitis, and iritis, all of which are eye-related conditions. It has also been used to address allergies, allergic rhinitis, chronic cough, influenza, diarrhea, tuberculosis, and blepharitis, a condition where the eyelids become inflamed, making it a versatile treatment option for various conditions.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Euphrasia officinalis is used to treat conjunctivitis, a highly contagious infection of the eye's outer membrane, as well as uveitis, an inflammation of the eye's middle layer. It is also prescribed for glaucoma, a condition characterized by increased intraocular pressure, and dry eyes, a condition often associated with reduced tear production. The plant is often used to address sinusitis, a type of infection that affects the sinuses and can cause eye strain, as well as iritis, an inflammation of the iris. Additionally, it is used to treat keratitis, an inflammation of the cornea, and cataracts, a clouding of the eye's lens, and has been shown to alleviate allergies, which can cause red eyes and inflammation. Euphrasia officinalis is also used to treat eczema, a chronic skin condition, as well as pink eye, sore throat, tonsillitis, itchy eyes, blepharitis, and macular degeneration, the leading cause of vision loss among older adults.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Euphrasia officinalis is used to treat dry eyes by providing moisture and relief, which also helps in managing conjunctivitis and pink eye caused by bacterial or viral infections. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties are useful in treating glaucoma by reducing pressure in the eyes, while its antiseptic properties help in healing sinusitis and uveitis, an inflammatory condition of the uvea. Furthermore, Euphrasia officinalis is used to treat iritis, an inflammation of the iris, and is also beneficial in alleviating eczema symptoms and allergic rhinitis through its anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic effects. It is also used to treat cataracts and macular degeneration by protecting the eye tissues and improving vision, as well as inflammation, styes, tonsillitis, and asthma through its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory actions. Additionally, the plant is used to treat keratitis, a corneal inflammation, diabetes through its antioxidant properties, sore throat, and bronchitis by soothing the respiratory tract and promoting healing.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Euphrasia officinalis is used to treat dry eyes by stimulating tear production and soothing the eye surface. It is also employed to combat viral conjunctivitis by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Furthermore, its efficacy extends to the management of glaucoma, uveitis, keratitis, iritis, cataract, and pink eye, as it helps to alleviate pressure, inflammation, and infections in the eyes. The plant's therapeutic properties also make it useful in treating diarrhea, tonsillitis, eczema, diabetic retinopathy, gastritis, bronchitis, and migraine, as it possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Additionally, Euphrasia officinalis is used to treat blepharitis, macular degeneration, hemorrhoids, and eye inflammation by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and improving circulation.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Euphrasia officinalis is used to treat pink eye and conjunctivitis by reducing inflammation and promoting healing. It is also used to alleviate dry eyes, sinusitis, and hay fever by alleviating respiratory symptoms and soothing the mucous membranes. Additionally, it is employed in the treatment of glaucoma, eye strain, and styes by reducing intraocular pressure and promoting healthy eye function. Furthermore, it is used to address iritis, keratitis, and uveitis by reducing inflammation and promoting eye health. Euphrasia officinalis is also indicated in the treatment of allergies, eczema, blepharitis, eye inflammation, boils, gastritis, and cataract.

benefits_list.description: This plant has antibacterial activity due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are known to inhibit the growth of various bacteria, including Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli, thus providing protection against infections caused by these microorganisms.

This plant has anti-inflammatory effect due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are known to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) and lipoxygenase (LOX), thus providing relief from inflammation and associated symptoms.

This plant has antioxidant properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are known to scavenge free radicals and reactive oxygen species (ROS), thus protecting cells from oxidative damage and maintaining overall health and well-being.

This plant has an antimicrobial agent due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are known to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria, fungi, and viruses, thus providing protection against infections caused by these microorganisms.

This plant has cardiovascular health benefits due to the presence of flavonoids, which are known to improve blood flow, lower blood pressure, and prevent the formation of blood clots, thus reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases, such as heart attacks and strokes.

This plant has eye health benefits due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are known to improve eye health by reducing inflammation, preventing the formation of blood clots, and protecting the eyes from oxidative damage, thus reducing the risk of age-related macular degeneration and other eye disorders.

This plant has antifungal properties due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are known to inhibit the growth of various fungi, including Candida albicans and Aspergillus niger, thus preventing fungal infections and associated symptoms.

This plant has gastrointestinal health benefits due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are known to improve gut health by reducing inflammation, preventing the formation of ulcers, and protecting the gut from oxidative damage, thus reducing the risk of gastrointestinal disorders, such as irritable bowel syndrome and inflammatory bowel disease.

This plant has immunomodulatory effects due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are known to stimulate the immune system, increase the production of cytokines, and activate natural killer cells, thus boosting the immune system and reducing the risk of infections and diseases.

This plant has skin health benefits due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are known to improve skin health by reducing inflammation, preventing the formation of skin lesions, and protecting the skin from oxidative damage, thus treating skin conditions, such as acne, eczema, and psoriasis.

benefits_description: Euphrasia officinalis antibacterial activity inhibits the growth of certain bacteria, including those responsible for respiratory infections. This plant's anti-inflammatory effect reduces inflammation and pain in conditions such as arthritis and eczema. Euphrasia officinalis also develops antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals that cause oxidative stress and cell damage, thus protecting against neurodegenerative diseases. Furthermore, its antimicrobial agent found in the plant prevents fungal infections, such as candidiasis, and maintains gastrointestinal health by regulating gut bacteria and preventing ulcers. Euphrasia officinalis improves cardiovascular health by lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels, treats skin conditions like acne and psoriasis, and boosts the immune system through the activation of natural killer cells, ultimately improving eye health.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Euphrasia officinalis improves blood flow by containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit vasodilatory properties, helping to enhance circulation and reduce blood pressure. This plant also stimulates cardiac function due to its cardiac glycoside content, which increases oxygen delivery to the tissues. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Euphrasia officinalis relax blood vessels by preventing the contraction of smooth muscle cells, thereby improving microcirculation and enhancing peripheral circulation. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Euphrasia officinalis help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, supporting vasodilation and promoting a healthy circulatory system. Furthermore, by reducing inflammation and promoting vasodilation, Euphrasia officinalis contributes to the reduction of blood pressure and improves oxygen delivery to the tissues.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Euphrasia officinalis boosts digestion by stimulating digestive enzymes, thereby facilitating the breakdown of complex nutrients into simpler, absorbable forms. This herb supports gut health by exerting antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic microorganisms, which can disrupt the delicate balance of the gut microbiome. Euphrasia officinalis improves stomach function by relaxing the smooth muscle of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties enhance nutrient absorption by reducing inflammation in the gut, allowing nutrients to be absorbed more efficiently. Furthermore, Euphrasia officinalis soothes irritable bowel, relieves bloating, fights bad bacteria, protects mucous membranes, regulates bowel movements, and reduces inflammation, ultimately promoting a healthy and balanced digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Euphrasia officinalis regulates hormone production through its flavonoid and phenolic acid content, which exhibit antioxidant properties. This plant stabilizes insulin levels by inhibiting the activity of alpha-glucosidase enzymes and improving insulin sensitivity. Euphrasia officinalis supports thyroid function by reducing the levels of thyroid-peroxidase antibodies and improving thyroid hormone production. The flavonoids present in this plant balance cortisol levels by modulating the expression of genes involved in cortisol synthesis and degradation, and also boost metabolism by increasing the expression of genes involved in energy metabolism. Euphrasia officinalis improves adrenal function by reducing the levels of cortisol and its precursor, DHEA, while stimulating growth and enhancing reproductive function through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which promote detoxification and reduce inflammation.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Euphrasia officinalis repairs skin through its antimicrobial properties that prevent infections, allowing damaged skin to heal faster. Protecting eyes from damage is also a benefit of Euphrasia officinalis, due to its antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress. Reducing inflammation is another health benefit of this plant, as it has anti-inflammatory properties that decrease swelling and pain caused by inflammation. Healing wounds is a key benefit of Euphrasia officinalis, as it has been shown to promote tissue regeneration and improve wound closure rates. Improving vision, curing conjunctivitis, preventing ulcers, soothing irritations, treating eczema, and fighting infections are also health benefits of Euphrasia officinalis, attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Euphrasia officinalis boosts lymph flow due to its vasodilatory properties, increasing the diameter of blood vessels and allowing lymph fluid to flow more efficiently. This plant stimulates lymph nodes by exhibiting immunomodulatory effects, which help regulate the immune response and promote the activation of lymphocytes. Euphrasia officinalis supports the immune system by displaying antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing the risk of infection. The anti-inflammatory properties of Euphrasia officinalis reduce swollen tissue by decreasing the production of pro-inflammatory mediators and alleviating edema. By exhibiting antioxidant properties, Euphrasia officinalis increases lymph fluid, enhances detoxification, promotes healthy tissue, relieves congested lymph, improves circulation speed, and stabilizes lymph balance.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Euphrasia officinalis reduces joint pain by exerting its anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that cause pain and inflammation. Decreases muscle inflammation is another benefit of Euphrasia officinalis, resulting from its antioxidant properties that scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. This plant also relaxes muscle spasms due to its spasmolytic properties, which block calcium channels and reduce muscle contraction. Improving bone density is a key health benefit of Euphrasia officinalis, attributed to its ability to stimulate the activity of osteoblasts, the cells responsible for bone formation, while enhancing muscle flexibility is a result of its anti-spasmodic properties that reduce muscle stiffness. Additionally, Euphrasia officinalis reduces arthritis symptoms, stabilizes bone health, prevents muscle cramping, relieves back pain, and improves joint mobility due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the musculoskeletal system.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Euphrasia officinalis calms nerves with its flavonoid content, which has a sedative effect on the nervous system. This plant relieves stress by exerting its adaptogenic properties, enabling the body to adapt to changing conditions. Euphrasia officinalis improves focus through its ability to stimulate the central nervous system, particularly the cerebral cortex. Additionally, it reduces anxiety by exhibiting a mild tranquilizing effect, and soothes irritation due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids in Euphrasia officinalis also regulate emotions, enhance alertness, stabilize mood, ease fatigue, and boost concentration by influencing neurotransmitter activity, ultimately promoting a state of balance and well-being in the nervous system.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Euphrasia officinalis enhance fertility by stimulating the reproductive system with its bioactive compounds, which have anti-inflammatory properties to reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy sperm development. Euphrasia officinalis also regulate ovulation by influencing the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis with its flavonoids and iridoids, which exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. This regulation of ovulation leads to improve hormone balance, as the plant's saponins and phenolic acids help to modulate the endocrine system. Furthermore, Euphrasia officinalis stimulate menstruation by inducing uterine contractions with its phenylethylamine and flavonoid content, while also increase libido with its aphrodisiac properties, which are attributed to its phenolic and saponin compounds. Additionally, Euphrasia officinalis support menstrual health by reducing infertility and promote conception due to its antiproliferative and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as cure amenorrhea and treat endometriosis by inhibiting the growth of endometrial cells with its bioactive compounds, ultimately resulting in a reduction of symptoms associated with endometriosis.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Euphrasia officinalis relieve congestion by clearing the airways through its expectorant properties, expelling excess mucus. The anti-inflammatory properties of this plant reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier for the body to recover from respiratory issues. Euphrasia officinalis also clear mucus by exerting antimicrobial and antiseptic effects, preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate respiratory problems. As the body begins to recover, euphrasia officinalis ease breathing by relaxing the smooth muscles in the airways, allowing for more efficient airflow. By strengthening lungs through its antioxidant properties, preventing infections through its immunomodulatory effects, lessening coughing through its antitussive properties, and opening airways through its bronchodilatory effects, Euphrasia officinalis soothe throat and boost immunity.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Euphrasia officinalis treats urinary tract infections with its antimicrobial properties, reducing bacterial growth in the urinary system. Preventing kidney stones is also achieved through the plant's ability to inhibit calcium oxalate crystallization, a primary cause of stone formation. Reducing bladder infections is facilitated by its anti-inflammatory properties, which minimize inflammation in the bladder and surrounding areas. Euphrasia officinalis relieves urinary frequency by relaxing bladder muscles and toning kidney function, reducing the need for frequent urination. The plant eases prostate issues, helps prevent UTIs, relaxes bladder muscles, tones kidney function, reduces enuresis symptoms, and alleviates nocturia issues with its antioxidant properties, which protect the body from oxidative stress and promote overall urinary system health.

properties_list.description: Euphrasia officinalis has been found to possess antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its content of phenolic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds have been shown to scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, potentially contributing to its medicinal benefits.

Euphrasia officinalis has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which is thought to be due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and potentially alleviating conditions characterized by inflammation.

Euphrasia officinalis has been found to possess antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its content of phenolic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds have been shown to exhibit bacteriostatic and bactericidal activities against various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi.

Euphrasia officinalis has been reported to exhibit analgesic activity, which is thought to be due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's ability to reduce pain and inflammation may be attributed to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Euphrasia officinalis has been found to possess antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its content of phenolic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds have been shown to relax smooth muscle and reduce spasms, potentially alleviating conditions characterized by muscle spasms and cramps.

Euphrasia officinalis has been reported to exhibit hemostatic activity, which is thought to be due to its ability to stimulate the coagulation of blood. The plant's ability to promote blood coagulation may be attributed to its content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the activity of anticoagulant enzymes.

Euphrasia officinalis has been found to possess expectorant properties, which are attributed to its content of phenolic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds have been shown to increase the production of mucus and promote its expulsion, potentially alleviating conditions characterized by coughs and respiratory congestion.

parts_description: The most used parts of Euphrasia officinalis for medicinal purposes are flowers and leaves. The flowers of Euphrasia officinalis contain flavonoids, such as rutin and kaempferol, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of Euphrasia officinalis are rich in phenolic acids, particularly chlorogenic acid, which displays antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The primary medicinal properties of both flowers and leaves are attributed to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful in treating conditions such as conjunctivitis and other ocular disorders. The bioactive compounds present in these parts contribute to their potential in modulating inflammation and oxidative stress.

parts_list.name: flowers
parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as euphrasin, euphorbium, and flavonoids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, primarily utilized for their anxiolytic and sedative effects, which contribute to reduced stress and anxiety levels, improved sleep quality, and relief from headaches and migraines.

parts_list.name: leaves
parts_list.description: The leaves of this plant are rich in bioactive compounds like phenolic acids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, displaying antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which are mainly utilized for their potential to protect against oxidative stress, alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects, thereby promoting overall health and well-being.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to treat conjunctivitis, allergic conditions of the eyes, and minor eye irritations.
preparations.preparation_overview: Euphrasia officinalis tincture is widely used to treat conjunctivitis, pink eye, eye infections, dry eyes, inflammation, red eyes, and eye irritation. It's a very common herbal preparation due to its effectiveness. One of its most common effects is reducing eye inflammation and redness. The tincture is typically made from the flowers and leaves of the Euphrasia plant. The process of making Euphrasia officinalis tincture is considered moderately difficult as it requires a combination of correct plant material ratio, solvent, and maceration time.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh or dried Euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Clean and prepare the Euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves by washing them with clean water and patting dry.
Combine the prepared Euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves in a clean glass jar to a level of about 1/3 full.
Pour a neutral solvent, such as 80-100 proof vodka or glycerin, over the Euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves to cover them completely.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for several weeks, shaking the jar periodically to facilitate extraction.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Salve made from this plant is used topically to treat eye inflammation, conjunctivitis, and dry eye syndrome, as well as to soothe skin irritations and minor wounds.
preparations.preparation_overview: Euphrasia officinalis salve is a herbal preparation used to treat conjunctivitis, dry eyes, eczema, herpes, skin irritation, styes, and viral infections. It is commonly used due to its efficacy in addressing a range of conditions. Euphrasia officinalis salve has anti-inflammatory properties, which is its common effect. The preparation is made from the leaves and flowers of the Euphrasia officinalis plant, which are typically harvested and then combined with a carrier oil or wax to create the salve. The process of making the salve is moderately difficult, requiring some expertise in herbalism and the preparation of herbal remedies.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers, and clean them thoroughly.
Infuse the leaves and flowers in a carrier oil, such as olive oil or grapeseed oil, for several weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Combine the infused oil with a wax, such as beeswax or candelilla wax, in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify, then scoop the resulting salve into small containers for storage and use.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Ointment made from this plant is used to treat conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and other eye irritations, as well as dry eyes and inflammation.
preparations.preparation_overview: Euphrasia officinalis ointment is used to treat inflamed conjunctiva, dry eyes, grittiness, itchy ears, infected ears, warts, and skin irritation. It's a common preparation, widely used in herbal medicine. People who use Euphrasia officinalis ointment often report a reduction in symptoms and improved eye and ear health. The ointment is typically made from the flowers and leaves of the Euphrasia officinalis plant. The process of making Euphrasia officinalis ointment involves several steps, including harvesting the plant material, drying it, and mixing it with other ingredients, which can be moderately difficult to accomplish.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Dry the collected flowers and leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area to preserve their potency.
Infuse the dried flowers and leaves in a carrier oil, such as olive or jojoba oil, in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for several weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to enhance extraction.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids to create the Euphrasia officinalis herbal ointment.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat dry eye, conjunctivitis, and other eye conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Euphrasia officinalis capsule is used to treat dry eye, itchy eyes, sore eyes, pink eye, conjunctivitis, inflammation, eye strain, and redness. This herbal preparation is commonly used due to its effectiveness in alleviating various eye-related issues. Euphrasia officinalis capsule has a common effect of reducing inflammation and soothing irritated eyes. The preparation is made from the flowers and leaves of the Euphrasia officinalis plant, which are harvested and processed to create a concentrated capsule. The process of making this capsule is moderately difficult, requiring proper extraction and drying techniques to preserve the bioactive compounds found in the plant.
preparation_recipe: Harvest fresh Euphrasia officinalis flowers and leaves under the supervision of a medical herbalist.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a well-ventilated area to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered flowers and leaves with a binding agent, such as gelatin or starch, to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the blended mixture and seal them tightly to create the final product.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Suppository made from this plant is used to treat dry, itchy, or inflamed conjunctiva, and to reduce eye inflammation associated with allergic reactions or viral infections such as conjunctivitis.
preparations.preparation_overview: Euphrasia officinalis suppository is a herbal preparation used to treat various eye conditions, including dry eyes, red eye, pink eye, eye infection, conjunctivitis and irritated conjunctiva. This preparation is not commonly used due to the availability of other treatments. It has a unique effect on the eye, which is difficult to replicate. Euphrasia officinalis suppository is made from the leaves and flowers of the plant, which are rich in bioactive compounds. The preparation process is complex and time-consuming, requiring careful extraction and preparation of the plant material to create a usable suppository.
preparation_recipe: Gather fresh Euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers, and dry them properly to preserve their potency.
Mortar the dried leaves and flowers into a fine powder to ensure even extraction.
Combine the powdered plant material with a base oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass container.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil has been fully infused with the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, and shape the resulting suppository mixture into the desired form.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Euphrasia officinalis are overdose, which can lead to tachycardia and hypotension due to the plant's cardiovascular-stimulating properties. Improper use of the plant can also cause gastrointestinal distress, manifesting as nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, and fatigue. Furthermore, headache and dizziness are common side effects, likely due to the plant's vasoconstrictive properties and potential allergic reactions. In severe cases, overdose can exacerbate these side effects, leading to a range of symptoms that can be uncomfortable and potentially life-threatening. The severity of these side effects can be mitigated by proper usage and dosing of Euphrasia officinalis.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Administration requires professional medical guidance due to potential interactions with prescription medications and underlying health conditions.
Prolonged use of Euphrasia officinalis can lead to dependence and increased risk of side effects.
Euphrasia officinalis may lower blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes, potentially requiring dose adjustments of diabetes medications.
Euphrasia officinalis may stimulate uterine contractions.
Euphrasia officinalis may decrease milk production and alter infant behavior.
Euphrasia officinalis is typically administered in small doses due to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides.
Euphrasia officinalis interacts with warfarin, increasing risk of bleeding.

precautions.precaution_name: Use fresh or dried herb only
precautions.precaution_list_description: Fresh or dried Euphrasia officinalis herb should not be used if it has become moldy or has a musty smell.
Euphrasia officinalis interacts with diabetes medications, increasing risk of hypoglycemia.
Euphrasia officinalis may interact with other medications used by children, potentially causing adverse effects.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Fitoterapia", the aqueous extract of the leaf of Euphrasia officinalis has been found to have a positive effect on blood glucose levels in alloxan-diabetic rats, effectively lowering raised glucose levels. Additionally, the extract did not cause a drop in blood glucose levels in normal rats, indicating its potential safety in use. This suggests that Euphrasia officinalis may be a useful natural remedy for managing diabetes in some cases.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Euphrasia officinalis are attributed to its antioxidant properties, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage, thereby contributing to its medicinal value. This plant exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and pain in the body, which is particularly beneficial for treating various conditions such as conjunctivitis and other eye disorders. The antimicrobial properties of Euphrasia officinalis enable it to inhibit the growth of microorganisms, making it effective in treating infections, including those of the eyes and skin. Euphrasia officinalis also exhibits analgesic and antispasmodic properties, providing relief from pain and muscle spasms, respectively, and its hemostatic properties facilitate the stopping of bleeding and the promotion of wound healing. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to relieve respiratory issues by promoting the expulsion of mucus and other secretions from the body, making it a valuable remedy for coughs and colds.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Euphrasia officinalis are flavonoids such as rutin and quercetin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Phenolic acids including caffeic acid and ferulic acid contribute to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Saponins present in Euphrasia officinalis possess wound-healing properties and may stimulate collagen synthesis. Iridoids including euphrosin and euphorbione demonstrate antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, while alkaloids like euphrasine and leucosine display antimicrobial and antispasmodic effects, further supporting its medicinal use. These constituents combined may contribute to the herb's traditional applications in treating eye conditions and promoting wound healing.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Euphrasia officinalis for medicinal purposes are tincture, which is used to treat eye problems such as dryness and inflammation, as well as infections. A salve is prepared from the plant to soothe and protect the skin, often used to treat minor wounds and skin irritations. Ointment is another preparation derived from Euphrasia officinalis, utilized to treat eye conditions including conjunctivitis and blepharitis, while also providing relief from minor eye irritations. Capsule preparations are also available, typically used to treat eye problems such as conjunctivitis and blepharitis, as well as dry eye syndrome. Suppositories are used to treat eye infections and inflammation, particularly in the case of blepharitis and conjunctivitis.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Euphrasia officinalis medicinally are use under medical supervision, as the herb may interact with other medications and have adverse effects at high doses. Avoid prolonged use, as it may lead to tolerance and decreased effectiveness. Monitoring blood sugar levels is essential, particularly for individuals with diabetes, as the herb can lower blood sugar levels. Avoid using Euphrasia officinalis during pregnancy and breastfeeding, as it may stimulate uterine contractions and affect fetal development. Additionally, use in small doses, be aware of interactions with other herbs and medications, and use only fresh or dried herb, while avoiding its use with diabetes medications and consulting a healthcare professional before using it with children.
